turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Participating in Group Decisions and Negotiating Responsibilities

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Participating in Group Decisions and Negotiating Responsibilities: “ Hello, our assignment is due next week. Can we decide who will do each part?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! What are the main parts of the assignment that we need to divide up?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, we can divide, I can start and them you can continuous

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, got it—you’ll start, and I’ll continue after you. Which part would you like to take first?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think I can start about introduction, then after you can talk about the main part and the middle part, and then together we can say about the conclusion.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, that works for me. I’ll handle the main and middle parts—does that include research and examples, or just explaining the ideas?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think you can include research and explain your idea. It's good.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, that sounds good to me. When would you like us to work together on the conclusion—before submission or on the due day?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: We can send submission.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, so we’ll submit it together. When exactly is the deadline, and how do we need to submit it—online or in class?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: On-line at 20/12

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, noted—online on the 20th of December. When would you like me to send you my part so you have time to review it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: You can send me... tomorrow?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, tomorrow works for me. What time would be best for you to receive it so you can review it comfortably?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Any time it’s good for me, thank you

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, perfect 👍 I’ll send you my part tomorrow then—should I send it by email or through our online platform?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes you can sendo for my email- lemes223@gmail.com

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, no problem. I’ll send it to lemes223@gmail.com tomorrow—do you want it as a Word document or a PDF?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, great. Just to confirm, which format do you prefer—Word document or PDF?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Word because I can editing

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect, that makes sense. I’ll send you a Word document to your email tomorrow—anything specific you want me to focus on in the research part?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, that’s ok

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, sounds good 👍 I’ll prepare the research and main sections and send the Word file tomorrow. If you think of any changes or extra ideas later, what’s the best way to let me know?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, sure. I think we can talk on the email

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, that works for me. I’ll email you tomorrow, and we can discuss any changes there—anything else you’d like to add before I start?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, thank so much!!

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re welcome! 😊 I’ll send everything tomorrow—talk to you by email. See you soon

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: See you!

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: See you! 👋 Talk to you soon.
